text.title: Launch Readiness Review Core Rules
text.summary: Cross-functional 8-gate pre-launch readiness review (security, performance, observability, runbooks, on-call, legal, support enablement, comms tree) producing a hard go/no-go decision with remediation list.

rule.statement: Review MUST evaluate all eight gates: security, performance, observability, runbooks, on-call, legal, support enablement, comms tree. Partial reviews are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Each gate maps to a known launch-failure mode; skipping any preserves the failure.

rule.statement: Each gate MUST have one named owner from the responsible function (not the PM by default). Owners sign the gate; un-owned gates fail the review.
rule.rationale: Diffuse ownership produces hand-waved sign-offs; one named owner per gate is auditable.

rule.statement: Each gate MUST evaluate against a hard written criterion (e.g., 'p95 latency < 250ms under expected load', 'runbook URL returns 200'). Qualitative 'feels ready' verdicts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Subjective sign-offs rubber-stamp easily; hard criteria force the actual check.

rule.statement: Any gate marked FAIL by its owner is a launch-blocker — no exec override without an explicit risk-acceptance memo on file. Yellow gates require remediation deadlines before launch.
rule.rationale: Without veto teeth, the readiness review becomes a ritual; weighting gates by veto power preserves the discipline.

rule.statement: Run readiness snapshots at T-7d, T-3d, T-1d. Each snapshot records gate status + open blockers. Drop-off between snapshots is the early-warning signal.
rule.rationale: Single-point readiness reviews mask late slippage; sequential snapshots surface drift in time to act.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — hotfix/minor release; internal beta; solo SaaS with lighter checklist; launch already shipped (use launch-retro)), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
